manifests.longDescription: Analyzes code using heuristic engines (ESLint, Ruff, etc.) and optionally LLM analyzers. Supports three modes: heuristic (CI, fast), full (local, comprehensive), llm (deep analysis).

flags.name: scope
flags.description: File scope: all, changed (vs main), staged

flags.name: repos
flags.description: Repository scope (submodule names to include, e.g., --repos kb-labs-core kb-labs-cli)

flags.name: task
flags.description: Task context - describe what the changes are trying to achieve

display.name: AI Review
display.description: AI-powered code review with heuristic engines (ESLint, Ruff, etc.) and LLM analyzers.

commands.longDescription: Analyzes code using heuristic engines (ESLint, Ruff, etc.) and optionally LLM analyzers. Supports three modes: heuristic (CI, fast), full (local, comprehensive), llm (deep analysis).

flags.name: scope
flags.description: File scope: all, changed (vs main), staged

flags.name: repos
flags.description: Repository scope (submodule names to include, e.g., --repos kb-labs-core kb-labs-cli)

flags.name: task
flags.description: Task context - describe what the changes are trying to achieve

manifests.longDescription: Unified agent command with multiple modes: execute (default) - run task immediately, plan - generate execution plan without running, edit - modify existing files, debug - analyze errors with trace context. Supports filesystem, search, shell, memory, and user interaction tools.

flags.name: mode
flags.description: Agent mode: execute (default), plan, edit, debug

flags.name: complexity
flags.description: Task complexity (for plan mode): simple, medium, complex

flags.name: execute
flags.description: After plan is approved, immediately execute it (use with --mode=plan --approve)

flags.name: dry-run
flags.description: Preview changes without applying (for edit mode)

flags.name: debug
flags.description: Enable debug mode: emit full prompts and responses as llm:debug events

flags.name: timeout
flags.description: Abort agent execution after this many seconds (exit code 124). No timeout by default.

flags.name: budget
flags.description: Override token budget (e.g. 300000 for heavy tasks). Overrides config value.

flags.name: json
flags.description: Output structured JSON result instead of human-readable output.

display.name: Agent System
display.description: Autonomous agent system with LLM tool calling, task orchestration, and tier escalation.

commands.longDescription: Unified agent command with multiple modes: execute (default) - run task immediately, plan - generate execution plan without running, edit - modify existing files, debug - analyze errors with trace context. Supports filesystem, search, shell, memory, and user interaction tools.

flags.name: mode
flags.description: Agent mode: execute (default), plan, edit, debug

flags.name: complexity
flags.description: Task complexity (for plan mode): simple, medium, complex

flags.name: execute
flags.description: After plan is approved, immediately execute it (use with --mode=plan --approve)

flags.name: dry-run
flags.description: Preview changes without applying (for edit mode)

flags.name: debug
flags.description: Enable debug mode: emit full prompts and responses as llm:debug events

flags.name: timeout
flags.description: Abort agent execution after this many seconds (exit code 124). No timeout by default.

flags.name: budget
flags.description: Override token budget (e.g. 300000 for heavy tasks). Overrides config value.

flags.name: json
flags.description: Output structured JSON result instead of human-readable output.

commands.describe: Show trace statistics with cost and performance metrics
commands.longDescription: Analyze trace file to show comprehensive statistics: iterations, LLM calls, token usage, tool usage, timing, and cost. Supports --json flag for AI agent consumption.

commands.describe: Filter trace events by type for debugging
commands.longDescription: Filter trace events by type (llm:call, tool:execution, error:captured, etc.). Use this to debug specific aspects of agent execution. Supports --json flag for programmatic access.

flags.name: type
flags.description: Event type to filter (llm:call, tool:execution, etc.)

commands.describe: View all events for a specific iteration
commands.longDescription: Show all trace events for a specific iteration number. Useful for debugging what happened in a particular loop iteration. Includes summary statistics and event timeline.

commands.describe: View what the LLM sees at each iteration — context window, truncations, responses
commands.longDescription: Shows the full context timeline for debugging agent behavior. For each LLM call: what messages are in the sliding window, what was truncated/dropped, and what the LLM responded with.

commands.describe: Quick diagnostic analysis — answers "what went wrong?" in one command
commands.longDescription: Comprehensive diagnostic report for agent execution. Analyzes errors, context window health (drops, truncations), tool failures, LLM reasoning text, loop detection, and quality indicators. One command to understand any agent issue.

commands.describe: Show quality control report for recent agent runs
commands.longDescription: Aggregates agent KPI telemetry from analytics buffer and shows quality, token usage, tool efficiency, drift, and regression alerts. Useful for continuous quality control and cost/performance monitoring.

commands.describe: Show file change history for agent sessions
commands.longDescription: View file changes made by agents during execution. Filter by session, file, or agent. Shows timestamps, operations, and change metadata.

commands.describe: Show diff for specific file change
commands.longDescription: Display line-by-line diff for a specific file change. Shows additions, deletions, and modifications with context.

flags.name: change-id
flags.description: Change ID to show diff for

commands.describe: Rollback file changes made by agents
commands.longDescription: Rollback file changes made by agents. Supports rollback by change ID, file path, agent ID, session, or timestamp. Use --dry-run to preview changes before applying.

flags.name: file
flags.description: File path to rollback all changes for

flags.name: agent-id
flags.description: Agent ID to rollback all changes by

flags.name: session-id
flags.description: Session ID to rollback all changes in

routes.description: Generate detailed spec from approved plan
List file changes for session (optionally filtered by runId)
Get unified diff for a specific file change
Rollback file changes for a session/run
Approve file changes for a session/run

channels.description: Persistent session stream (all runs in session)

artifacts.description: Persistent agent memory (facts, sessions, project context).

commands.longDescription: Rollback file changes made by agents. Supports rollback by change ID, file path, agent ID, session, or timestamp. Use --dry-run to preview changes before applying.

flags.name: file
flags.description: File path to rollback all changes for

flags.name: agent-id
flags.description: Agent ID to rollback all changes by

flags.name: session-id
flags.description: Session ID to rollback all changes in

List file changes for session (optionally filtered by runId)
Get unified diff for a specific file change

Approve file changes for a session/run

channels.description: Persistent session stream (all runs in session)

manifests.longDescription: View file changes made by agents during execution. Filter by session, file, or agent. Shows timestamps, operations, and change metadata.

display.name: Agent System
display.description: Autonomous agent system with LLM tool calling, task orchestration, and tier escalation.

commands.longDescription: Unified agent command with multiple modes: execute (default) - run task immediately, plan - generate execution plan without running, edit - modify existing files, debug - analyze errors with trace context. Supports filesystem, search, shell, memory, and user interaction tools.

flags.name: mode
flags.description: Agent mode: execute (default), plan, edit, debug

flags.name: complexity
flags.description: Task complexity (for plan mode): simple, medium, complex

flags.name: execute
flags.description: After plan is approved, immediately execute it (use with --mode=plan --approve)

flags.name: dry-run
flags.description: Preview changes without applying (for edit mode)

flags.name: debug
flags.description: Enable debug mode: emit full prompts and responses as llm:debug events

flags.name: timeout
flags.description: Abort agent execution after this many seconds (exit code 124). No timeout by default.

flags.name: budget
flags.description: Override token budget (e.g. 300000 for heavy tasks). Overrides config value.

flags.name: json
flags.description: Output structured JSON result instead of human-readable output.

commands.describe: Show trace statistics with cost and performance metrics
commands.longDescription: Analyze trace file to show comprehensive statistics: iterations, LLM calls, token usage, tool usage, timing, and cost. Supports --json flag for AI agent consumption.

commands.describe: Filter trace events by type for debugging
commands.longDescription: Filter trace events by type (llm:call, tool:execution, error:captured, etc.). Use this to debug specific aspects of agent execution. Supports --json flag for programmatic access.

flags.name: type
flags.description: Event type to filter (llm:call, tool:execution, etc.)

commands.describe: View all events for a specific iteration
commands.longDescription: Show all trace events for a specific iteration number. Useful for debugging what happened in a particular loop iteration. Includes summary statistics and event timeline.

commands.describe: View what the LLM sees at each iteration — context window, truncations, responses
commands.longDescription: Shows the full context timeline for debugging agent behavior. For each LLM call: what messages are in the sliding window, what was truncated/dropped, and what the LLM responded with.

commands.describe: Quick diagnostic analysis — answers "what went wrong?" in one command
commands.longDescription: Comprehensive diagnostic report for agent execution. Analyzes errors, context window health (drops, truncations), tool failures, LLM reasoning text, loop detection, and quality indicators. One command to understand any agent issue.

commands.describe: Show quality control report for recent agent runs
commands.longDescription: Aggregates agent KPI telemetry from analytics buffer and shows quality, token usage, tool efficiency, drift, and regression alerts. Useful for continuous quality control and cost/performance monitoring.

commands.describe: Show file change history for agent sessions
commands.longDescription: View file changes made by agents during execution. Filter by session, file, or agent. Shows timestamps, operations, and change metadata.

commands.describe: Show diff for specific file change
commands.longDescription: Display line-by-line diff for a specific file change. Shows additions, deletions, and modifications with context.

flags.name: change-id
flags.description: Change ID to show diff for

manifests.longDescription: Analyzes changes, generates commit plan with LLM, applies commits locally. Use --dry-run to preview without applying, --with-push to push after applying.

flags.name: scope
flags.description: Filter by package name (@kb-labs/core), wildcard (@kb-labs/*), or path pattern (packages/**)

flags.name: dry-run
flags.description: Generate plan only, do not apply

display.name: Commit Generator
display.description: AI-powered commit generation with conventional commit support.

commands.longDescription: Analyzes changes, generates commit plan with LLM, applies commits locally. Use --dry-run to preview without applying, --with-push to push after applying.

flags.name: scope
flags.description: Filter by package name (@kb-labs/core), wildcard (@kb-labs/*), or path pattern (packages/**)

flags.name: dry-run
flags.description: Generate plan only, do not apply

commands.describe: Generate commit plan from git changes.
commands.longDescription: Analyzes staged and unstaged changes using git diff, then uses LLM to group related changes and generate conventional commit messages.

flags.name: scope
flags.description: Filter by package name (@kb-labs/core), wildcard (@kb-labs/*), or path pattern (packages/**)

commands.describe: Apply current commit plan (create local commits).
commands.longDescription: Creates git commits according to the current plan. Checks for staleness (working tree changes since plan generation) unless --force is used.

flags.name: force
flags.description: Apply even if working tree changed

commands.longDescription: Pushes local commits to the remote. Refuses force push to protected branches (main, master) by default.
Displays the current commit plan if one exists.
Removes the current commit plan from storage.

artifacts.description: Git status snapshot at plan generation time.

manifests.longDescription: Performs a health check on the workflow daemon by making an HTTP request to /health endpoint. Use this to verify the daemon is running and responding.

display.name: Workflow CLI
display.description: CLI commands for interacting with KB Labs Workflow Daemon.

commands.longDescription: Performs a health check on the workflow daemon by making an HTTP request to /health endpoint. Use this to verify the daemon is running and responding.
Fetches comprehensive metrics from the workflow daemon including total runs, queued jobs, running jobs, completed jobs, and failure counts.

commands.describe: Get status of a specific workflow job.
commands.longDescription: Retrieves detailed status information for a specific job by ID, including current state, start time, and completion time if finished.

flags.name: job-id
flags.description: Job ID to get status for

commands.describe: Get logs for a specific workflow job.
commands.longDescription: Fetches execution logs for a specific job by ID. Note: Log integration with platform.logger is pending, currently returns placeholder data.

flags.name: job-id
flags.description: Job ID to get logs for (required)

commands.longDescription: Lists all currently active workflow executions or cron jobs. Can be filtered by status (running, queued, completed, failed, cancelled) or type (runs, cron).

commands.describe: Submit a raw job for execution.
commands.longDescription: Submits a job to the workflow daemon for execution. Requires a handler (plugin command) and optionally accepts input parameters as JSON. Can wait for job completion with --wait flag.
Runs a workflow definition by workflow ID via /api/v1/workflows/:id/run endpoint. Supports request-level target and isolation overrides.

flags.name: input
flags.description: JSON string of workflow input payload

flags.name: isolation
flags.description: Isolation profile: strict, balanced, or relaxed

flags.name: trigger-type
flags.description: Trigger type: manual, api, or cron

routes.describe: Get dashboard statistics
List all workflow definitions
Get workflow definition details
Run a workflow
Get workflow run history with pagination
Cancel a running or queued workflow run
List all workflow runs across all workflows
Get detailed information about a specific workflow run
List workflow jobs with optional filters
Get detailed information about a specific job
Get execution logs for a specific job
Get execution steps and progress for a specific job
Cancel a running or pending job
List all registered cron jobs
List steps waiting for approval in a workflow run
Approve or reject a pending approval step

flags.name: job-id
flags.description: Job ID to get logs for (required)

commands.longDescription: Lists all currently active workflow executions or cron jobs. Can be filtered by status (running, queued, completed, failed, cancelled) or type (runs, cron).

commands.describe: Submit a raw job for execution.
commands.longDescription: Submits a job to the workflow daemon for execution. Requires a handler (plugin command) and optionally accepts input parameters as JSON. Can wait for job completion with --wait flag.
Runs a workflow definition by workflow ID via /api/v1/workflows/:id/run endpoint. Supports request-level target and isolation overrides.

flags.name: input
flags.description: JSON string of workflow input payload

flags.name: isolation
flags.description: Isolation profile: strict, balanced, or relaxed

flags.name: trigger-type
flags.description: Trigger type: manual, api, or cron

routes.describe: Get dashboard statistics
List all workflow definitions
Get workflow definition details
Run a workflow
Get workflow run history with pagination
Cancel a running or queued workflow run
List all workflow runs across all workflows
Get detailed information about a specific workflow run
List workflow jobs with optional filters
Get detailed information about a specific job
Get execution logs for a specific job
Get execution steps and progress for a specific job
Cancel a running or pending job
List all registered cron jobs
List steps waiting for approval in a workflow run
Approve or reject a pending approval step

manifests.describe: Get logs for a specific workflow job.
manifests.longDescription: Fetches execution logs for a specific job by ID. Note: Log integration with platform.logger is pending, currently returns placeholder data.

flags.name: job-id
flags.description: Job ID to get logs for (required)

display.name: Workflow CLI
display.description: CLI commands for interacting with KB Labs Workflow Daemon.

commands.longDescription: Performs a health check on the workflow daemon by making an HTTP request to /health endpoint. Use this to verify the daemon is running and responding.
Fetches comprehensive metrics from the workflow daemon including total runs, queued jobs, running jobs, completed jobs, and failure counts.

commands.describe: Get status of a specific workflow job.
commands.longDescription: Retrieves detailed status information for a specific job by ID, including current state, start time, and completion time if finished.

flags.description: Job ID to get status for
